steps.title: Identify the support relationship
steps.content: The text first says Lenoir and Durand love cats. The next sentence says they created Stray, a game that lets players explore a city as a clever orange house cat. That detail gives a concrete example of their love of cats, so the transition should show reinforcement or emphasis rather than a shift in time or contrast.

steps.title: Reject the mismatched transitions
steps.content: Choice A (Intermittently,) suggests something happening at intervals, which does not describe the relationship between loving cats and making a cat-centered game. Choice B (On the other hand,) and Choice D (Nevertheless,) both signal contrast, but the game supports the previous claim instead of opposing it. The needed transition introduces stronger evidence for the same idea. The answer is C.
